evals.prompt: Build a cinematic AI SaaS landing page for 'Lumina AI' — an AI writing assistant. Dark theme, indigo palette. Use video background hero, native CSS scroll-driven animations, liquid glass components, bento feature grid, and pricing section. Stack: Next.js 15 + Tailwind + Framer Motion.
evals.expected_output: A complete dark-theme landing page: OKLCH :root tokens (not HSL/hex), .liquid-glass utility, <video> hero with gradient overlay, eyebrow badge, oversized gradient headline, Framer Motion per-element stagger, CSS @keyframes marquee with duplicated array, native animation-timeline: view() for scroll reveals, bento feature grid, liquid-glass pricing cards, @starting-style on any modals, @view-transition navigation, and zero hardcoded hex values.
expectations: Defines :root token system using OKLCH format — not HSL, not hex
Implements .liquid-glass utility class with backdrop-filter blur(20px) saturate(180%) and inner top highlight using oklch(100% 0 0 / 0.15)
Hero uses <video autoPlay muted loop playsInline> with absolute inset-0
Hero video has gradient overlay using oklch bg values — not hex
Hero content layer is z-10 relative to video background
Eyebrow badge uses liquid-glass + rounded-full + animated dot
Headline uses clamp() for fluid font sizing and negative letter-spacing (≤ -0.03em)
Headline uses OKLCH gradient text via bg-clip-text text-transparent
Primary CTA button has glow box-shadow using oklch color values
Logo marquee uses CSS @keyframes marquee with duplicated brand array for seamless loop
Logo marquee uses mask-image fade edges on container
Logo pills use liquid-glass styling
Features section uses bento grid layout — cards have varied sizes, not identical grid
Pricing section has at least 2 tiers with liquid-glass card styling
Featured pricing tier has gradient border or stronger glow
Framer Motion animations specify easing as cubic-bezier — not named strings like 'easeOut'
Scroll reveals use animation-timeline: view() — NOT Intersection Observer or JS scroll listener
@starting-style rule present for at least one enter animation (modal, toast, or dialog)
@view-transition { navigation: auto } present in global CSS
Grain texture overlay present on body using SVG fractalNoise (opacity 0.025–0.045)
Zero hardcoded hex values in any component or CSS file
No lorem ipsum — realistic AI product copy throughout
Mobile responsive with no horizontal overflow at 375px

evals.prompt: Build a professional fintech expense dashboard for 'Clearpath'. Light mode, professional OKLCH blue palette. Sidebar nav, top bar, 4 KPI cards, spend chart, filterable transactions table. Use CSS Container scroll-state for sticky sidebar shadow. Make it look like a Y-Combinator fintech company product.
evals.expected_output: A light-mode fintech dashboard: OKLCH token system (light bg L>95%), sidebar with scroll-state sticky shadow, KPI cards using --ok and --err semantic tokens for trend indicators, chart, table with realistic financial data, skeleton loaders, empty state, light-mode liquid-glass, mobile-responsive sidebar, @starting-style on drawers, zero hex values.
expectations: Defines :root OKLCH token system — background is near-white (L ≥ 95%)
Primary color is blue hue OKLCH (H ≈ 265) — professional, not purple or green
Border radius is conservative 4–10px — enterprise/fintech feel
Sidebar uses CSS container scroll-state or intersection observer for sticky shadow
Sidebar navigation has icon + label pattern with at least 2 section groups
Top bar includes search input and user avatar
Exactly 4 KPI metric cards visible with properly labeled metrics
KPI trend uses --ok OKLCH token (green) for positive and --err OKLCH token (red) for negative
Chart component present for spend visualization (recharts, chart.js, or SVG)
Transactions table with filter or sort — realistic financial data
Data includes real-sounding company names and plausible dollar amounts
Skeleton loading state using CSS animation (pulse) for at least one section
Empty state with friendly copy and CTA action
Light-mode liquid-glass uses oklch(100% 0 0 / 0.82) or similar with dark border
NO gradient text on headlines — plain bold text for trust
NO neon glow effects — shadows are subtle, desaturated
@starting-style on any drawer or panel that slides in
Sidebar collapses to drawer or hidden at mobile breakpoint (<1024px)
Zero hardcoded hex values in component files

evals.prompt: Build a portfolio for freelance designer 'Aria Chen'. Bold editorial style, warm dark OKLCH palette (copper/amber primary), oversized kinetic typography, grain texture. Use native CSS scroll-driven animations for reveals. Gallery grid, about section, contact form, blog listing. Pure HTML/CSS/Vanilla JS — no frameworks.
evals.expected_output: Multi-section portfolio in pure HTML/CSS/JS: OKLCH :root tokens (warm dark values), oversized serif/display headline mixing italic+upright weights, CSS scroll-driven animation-timeline: view() for reveals, @starting-style for contact form modal, grain texture, gallery grid with hover effects, about section, contact form with validation, blog listing, responsive at 375px+1440px, zero frameworks.
expectations: Uses only HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript — no npm packages, no frameworks
CSS :root defines OKLCH color tokens with warm dark background (H ≈ 40–60)
Primary/accent is warm OKLCH: copper, amber, or gold hue
Grain texture overlay using CSS body::after with SVG fractalNoise filter at opacity 0.03–0.05
Hero headline is oversized (clamp min ≥ 3.5rem) with tight leading (≤ 1.05)
Headline mixes italic and upright font weights for editorial contrast
Scroll reveals use animation-timeline: view() — NOT addEventListener scroll
@starting-style used for contact form modal or confirmation dialog
Grain texture is CSS-only using SVG filter in background-image data URI
Project gallery uses CSS Grid with hover effects (scale, overlay, or clip-path reveal)
About section has realistic bio with personality for Aria Chen
Contact form has proper labels, input types, and client-side validation
Blog listing shows at least 3 posts with title, date, reading time, and excerpt
Responsive works at 375px mobile and 1440px desktop — no horizontal overflow
No lorem ipsum — copy has creative design portfolio personality

evals.prompt: Build a developer tool hero for 'Axiom' — an observability platform. Dark green OKLCH terminal aesthetic. Monospace font. Include a terminal block showing npm install output with staggered line reveal. Use sibling-index() for stagger if supported, fall back to :nth-child delays. React + Tailwind.
evals.expected_output: Developer tool hero with OKLCH dark green tokens, .liquid-glass with darker tint, monospace font, terminal block (liquid-glass card, title-bar dots, npm install lines with stagger reveal), solid green headline (not gradient), sharp radius (≤ 8px), marquee with dev ecosystem brands, monospace CTA buttons, @starting-style on terminal block appear, zero hex.
expectations: Defines :root OKLCH tokens with green primary (H ≈ 140–160)
Background has green undertone — not neutral dark gray
Primary font is monospace (JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, Geist Mono, or similar)
Liquid-glass uses lower opacity (≤ 0.06) — darker tint for dev aesthetic
Navbar includes npm install copy-pill in liquid-glass styling with monospace font
Hero background uses scanline texture (repeating-linear-gradient) or dot grid
Terminal block present: liquid-glass card with title-bar colored dots and monospace lines
Terminal title bar has 3 dots using --err --warn --ok OKLCH colors
Terminal lines show: npm install command + success response at minimum
Terminal lines reveal with stagger using sibling-index() or :nth-child animation-delay
@starting-style applied to terminal block entering the page
Headline uses solid green primary color — NOT gradient (terminal aesthetic)
Border radius is sharp (2–8px) — no rounded-full on cards
Logo marquee has fictional developer ecosystem brand names
CTA buttons use monospace font
Zero hardcoded hex values in components

evals.prompt: Build a pricing page for 'Forma AI' — a design SaaS tool. Three tiers: Free ($0), Pro ($29/mo or $24/mo yearly), Enterprise (custom). Monthly/yearly toggle with Framer layoutId price animation. Dark OKLCH background. Liquid-glass cards. Pro featured. FAQ accordion with CSS @starting-style expand animation.
evals.expected_output: Dark SaaS pricing page: OKLCH :root tokens, .liquid-glass utility, monthly/yearly toggle (liquid-glass with animated pill), Pro card with gradient border and Most Popular badge, Framer layoutId price number animation, AnimatePresence for savings badge, feature checklists, differentiated CTAs, FAQ accordion with @starting-style height animation, zero hex values.
expectations: Defines :root OKLCH token system
Implements .liquid-glass utility using oklch color values
Section header has eyebrow liquid-glass pill + headline + subheadline
Monthly/Yearly toggle uses liquid-glass with animated active pill indicator
Yearly option shows savings badge using OKLCH warn color (amber)
Exactly 3 pricing tiers: Free, Pro, Enterprise
Pro card has gradient border using OKLCH primary values
Pro card has 'Most Popular' badge positioned absolutely
Price numbers use Framer Motion layoutId for smooth number morphing on toggle
AnimatePresence wraps 'Save $X/year' text below Pro price (appears on yearly)
Each tier has feature checklist with checkmarks using OKLCH ok token (green)
Pro features include 'Everything in Starter +' pattern
CTA buttons differ: ghost for Free, primary+glow for Pro, text for Enterprise
Enterprise shows 'Talk to sales' — no numeric price displayed
FAQ accordion below pricing cards with at least 6 questions
FAQ expand/collapse uses @starting-style for smooth height transition
@view-transition { navigation: auto } in global CSS
Zero hardcoded hex values in any component

evals.prompt: Build an Awwwards-quality hero for 'Forma Studio' — a creative design agency. Neo-brutalism aesthetic. Warm dark OKLCH palette. Oversized mixed-weight editorial typography. Zero radius. Grain texture. GSAP ScrollTrigger parallax. Asymmetric layout — NOT a standard left-text right-image hero. React + Tailwind + GSAP.
evals.expected_output: Neo-brutalist agency hero: OKLCH warm dark tokens (copper/amber primary), liquid-glass with warm tint using OKLCH amber, oversized serif/display headline (≥80px) mixing italic+black weights, tight leading (≤1.05), zero border radius, grain texture, GSAP character reveal on headline, ScrollTrigger parallax on image, custom cursor, horizontal divider as design element, no gradient text, creative personality copy.
expectations: OKLCH tokens: warm dark background (H ≈ 40–60), warm primary (copper/amber H ≈ 45–65)
Liquid-glass uses warm OKLCH tint: oklch(85% 0.08 55 / 0.08) gradient
Display font is serif, bold-display, or heavy geometric (Clash Display, Playfair, etc.)
Headline is oversized: minimum 80px at desktop, clamp() for fluid sizing
Headline leading is tight: ≤ 1.05
Headline mixes italic (font-style: italic) and upright weights
Layout is asymmetric — does NOT use standard left-text right-image two-column format
Border radius is 0 (zero) throughout — intentional neo-brutalism
Grain texture overlay present on body using SVG fractalNoise filter
Horizontal rule or decorative hr element used as intentional design element
GSAP GSAP.from() or SplitText used for headline character or word reveal
GSAP ScrollTrigger present for at least one parallax element
Custom cursor element present with CSS mix-blend-mode: difference
Primary headline does NOT use gradient text — editorial restraint
Copy has creative agency personality — not generic SaaS voice
Zero hardcoded hex values in components

evals.prompt: Build a SaaS project management dashboard for 'Tasked'. Full OKLCH token system audit. Sidebar nav, top bar, 4 KPI cards (Active Projects/Tasks Due/Team Members/Completion Rate), area chart, task table, team member list. Next.js 15 + Tailwind. Prove zero hardcoded hex — all colors from OKLCH tokens.
evals.expected_output: Project management dashboard with comprehensive OKLCH :root system (all semantic tokens), sidebar with section groups, top bar, 4 labeled KPI cards with trend colors from semantic tokens, area chart, task table with status chips, team list, skeleton loaders, empty state, @starting-style on any entering panels, mobile-responsive sidebar collapse, and provably zero hardcoded hex values.
expectations: Complete :root OKLCH token system with: --bg --surface --surface-up --fg --fg-muted --fg-dim --primary --primary-up --ok --warn --err --border --border-faint
All background properties use oklch(var(--bg)) or oklch(var(--surface))
All text colors use oklch(var(--fg)) or oklch(var(--fg-muted))
All border properties use oklch(var(--border))
All border-radius values use var(--r) or var(--r-*) tokens
All animation-duration uses var(--dur-*) tokens
All transition-timing-function uses var(--ease-*) tokens
Zero hardcoded hex values (#RRGGBB) in any component or CSS file
Zero hardcoded hsl() values in components — all converted to oklch()
Zero hardcoded rgba() values — all use oklch(... / opacity) syntax
Sidebar navigation has icon + label and at least 2 labeled section groups
Top bar has search input and user avatar or profile button
Exactly 4 KPI cards: Active Projects, Tasks Due Today, Team Members, Completion Rate
KPI trend uses oklch(var(--ok)) for positive and oklch(var(--err)) for negative
Area or line chart present for task or project progress visualization
Task table has columns for task name, assignee, status chip, and due date
Status chips use oklch() tinted backgrounds matching chip category
Team member list shows avatar, name, and role
Skeleton loading state defined with CSS pulse animation for at least cards section
Empty state component defined with icon, copy, and primary CTA
@starting-style applied to any panel, drawer, or modal in the dashboard
Sidebar collapses to drawer or hidden on mobile (<1024px)
